Spirit Talker's War Lance  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 30
DMG: 25 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +8 WIS: +16 HP: +15 MANA: +45
SV MAGIC: +6
WT: 7.5 Size: LARGE
Class: SHM
Race: BAR ELF TRL OGR IKS VAH FRG
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Post Comment
ubah sham tank
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2004 at 6:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771345677
View User Forum
28 posts
I dont care what anyone sayd about shamans not being able to tank! I have a 65 shaman with some decent gear...some elemental some not. I can easily hang in there and take aggro from a caster class or help the tanks by tanking an add. I have self buffed and virt only stats of 305 on all but cha and int and have 6,732 hp and 1217 ac. yes this is ubah for a shaman. you tell me a tank that has that much hp and ac cant tank, and ill tell you to(well we wont go there). anyway with self haste or chanty haste i can solo or tank a lot of mobs. we shamans can overcome more than most melees or have not as many issues when it come to tanking, if geared right. we can slow cripple heal and have a pet to aid us, not to mention buff ourselves, haste, HOT, and then if need to med we canni, and get back mana faster than any caster class. i could go on and on but as you i wont bore anyone anymore, we shamans are mean and if geared right probably the best pvpers/duelers in the game.(save the facsomeones resists are not out the dang roof)

# Feb 18 2004 at 6:00 AM Rating: Decent

4 posts
howabout a location where this mob is please.

Dont Bother reading this thread
QuoteReply
# Sep 02 2003 at 12:05 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591787124229
16 posts
This thread is full of people discussing melee vs casting. Don't bother scrolling past this post if you're interested in

FINDING OUT SOMETHING ABOUT THE ITEM!!!
▲ Page top
nice poker
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2003 at 1:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248423104
View User Forum
50 posts
Hi there. I was just looking over the DN gear again, and looking at this very nice shaman spear, and of course reading all of the posts, and decided "who cares if you melee or not with this, its still nice". So, for those of you who care, yes, at 52 1/2, I do melee quite often as a shaman, whether solo or in a group, UNLESS I am the main healer (for whatever insane reason). With turgur's, an ebolt, alacrity, HoS, agility, stamina, and (ooooo, I hate when i forget names!) my ac buff, I do fairly well in melee. With Dawg along, I do even better. If I am in a group, regardless of if I am main healer or not, I ALWAYS keep enough mana for 2-4 sup heals because I might just have to save the MT or the cleric, or the chanter from some violent death. Do I try to gain aggro? Nope. I have /pet back off and /pet kill hotkeyed and HE goes and starts taunting on things, while I try to keep someone alive. Doesn't always work, but thats the game I guess. I go thru a lot of dawgs sometimes, but he can taunt and I can't, and sometimes the melee are busy. I rarely melee in zones that are very laggy for me, since I just keep looking at the ground and concentrate on slows and dots and heals. But, if I am soloing, of course I have to melee, cause lets face it, at 52, yer dawg is LB con to yourself at best, and while he he hits when buffed for 40ish, he doesn't have the hp I do buffed, and he needs help from time to time. So, yah, shaman, cleric, druid melee is dependant on the situation, conditions, necessity, and just plain old personal choice.

so, back to the spear. I don't have better than this, no epic yet. I wear my old sweatstained totemic helm cause frankly, there isn't much better out there for ac and wis for a shaman until you get the spirittalkers headdress and the thurg/kael and up armors. My best weapon is my Howling Harpoon for when i choose to melee (its in my ranged slot right now for bonuses, but I might take my spear of foresight back from rogue to go there and keep howling in primary) with spine chill spear in primary right now. So, I think tief is gonna get the spinechill and i'll take my spear of foresight back. At 52 1/2, I would love to have this weapon, however I don't know if I would want to give up a shield with stats for this. Hehe, I finally got my stinkin' strathbone shell shield from kaesora (lol, only took me 30 levels of trying!) and keep my old cgs in the bank. but, on the other hand, the wis bonus on this is a bit better than what I have now, plus other stats. But, lol. This is the kind of thing you can go on forever with, and If I don't shut up now, I will. This is nice for anyone who gets it, I don't care if you hang it up on the wall or poke everything to death with it. Its nice, and if you get it, you deserve it, cause while I haven't been to DN yet, or even know of anyone with this poker, from what I read here and ahve heard of DN, its difficult to get. Y'all take care, and stop flamin each other :)

Wotjan Wynterheart 52 Barbarian Shaman, Druzzil Ro, Darkstorm Dragons
▲ Page top
Shamans Rock
QuoteReply
# Nov 19 2002 at 1:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776234042
5 posts
I have no choice but to help defend shamans. I play a 51 shaman on Povar. My gear is decent and with self buffs i sit just under 1500hp and just under 800ac. Stats str 253 sta around 160 agi 180ish dex 180ish wis 228 mana just under 2500.When i group I use Crystal Chitin shield and Spine chill. That is until we get in trouble or Im at FM (or near fm)Then I grab Barb Spiritualist hammer (hit regularly 45ish tops 80ish). Everyone forgets that yes mobs at my lvl can dbl for 130dmg, but hey when slowed by 70% who cares. More then enough time to root and back away to let tanks grab aggro. If my cleric or enchanter gets in trouble, ***** medding I save their a@@ fast. Shamans can tank for short periods, we can melee fairly well, and our buffs help clerics get big butts from sitting around. If you really think a shaman cant melee, your right, we are clerics, but if you pull 5 mobs and 1 breaks mezz and goes after enchanter, who can grab aggro fast to get chanty back to mezzing? (Shamans....cuz we can hit damn hard and make you about as fast as a herd of turtles stampeding through peanut butter) BTW I solo giants in GD all the time and yes I TANK!!!when you slow a mob like we can he cant hit you now cn he?

#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 09 2002 at 5:09 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This stuff makes me laugh. Let's cover some facts.
RE: meelee
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2002 at 12:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247806950
12 posts
Everybody is an expert! =/

Yes Tragic, you must know everything to know about EQ there is! I will be the first to buy your book "EQ the Tragic Way" I have changed my ways of playing the shaman to only sitting when I'm not slowing mobs. Since this is how you say we should play, and Tragic, you know it all man! And to no surprise I'll prolly find out that you created EQ and that's how you inteded shaman's to be.... well I mean that's the only way that you would know all this. Right?
▲ Page top
RE: meelee
QuoteReply
# Sep 12 2002 at 11:05 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591758215454
100 posts
Tragic, Your pathetic attempt at debunking a class due to your inate jealousy of it is rather stupid. Let me lay low some of your inaccuracies from my own perspective. That of a 58 Shaman.


Hmm, where to start...... Ahh, okies, buffs. Well, self buffed I get to a 255str, 220 sta, 198 agi, and a 238 dex. This places my Atk rating well over the 1k mark and my Damage per swing very nice. Let's use the example of the two most common weapon Shaman use post 55+. The Rune Etched Icewrum Fang and The Spear of Fate.


Ran a test on this and self hasted and buffed using Spear of Fate I swing 6 times every 5 seconds and connect on most mobs 4 times. That leaves me with 4 hits every 5 seconds and that equals to 48 hits a minute. Now With the average damage of 44 points per swing, Yes, That's the average, High being 80+ low being 4-5, that equates to 2112 points on a minute. That equates to roughly 35 DPS. I don't find that very shabby myself.

Now let's take the Rune Etched Icewurm Fang. This puppy in nuts. With a 12/19 and self hasted I can swing insanely fast. This roughly works out to about 2 swings every second. 120 swings in a minute and I still hit for an average 80 times a minutes. Average damage for these contacts is 23 points, Max is 48, minimum being 1. 1840 damage per minute or 30 dps. Now that is also not counting in the very nice procing ratio of the fang for 81 points of damage. This simply adds to my dps.

Now Tragic, if you don't feel that 2100 points of damage on top of me slowing, crippleing, adding my 2k points of Dots, and having my hasted, buffed pet Bash, Kick, and double hit mobs is meaningful, productive, or important, all the while tossing out extra heals, and buffs, post 55+ on mobs that are damn near gaurenteed to last longer than a minute in combat, you sir need to get your head out of your *** and quit thinking you know all about what every class can do.


So do us a favor and keep your small minded, pathetic, class envying comments to yourself.

Oh, and that's coming from an Iksar Shaman who can't wear a fookin JBB.

I NEVER go oom btw.
▲ Page top
RE: meelee
QuoteReply
# Oct 05 2002 at 6:21 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592759422721
39 posts
First off, I am not commenting on whether or not Shaman can melee or not. What I am doing is pointing out a false fact in your statement.

"Now let's take the Rune Etched Icewurm Fang. This puppy in nuts. With a 12/19 and self hasted I can swing insanely fast. This roughly works out to about 2 swings every second."

Not possible. 2 swings every second means a delay ratio of roughly 5. No matter what you do, hasted or not hasted, your delay cannot and will not drop below 11. The only case in which this is possible is with the Moss Covered Twig, which starts with 10 delay, but was taken out of the game for that very reason. So no, you cannot swing twice every second. Maybe if you were dual wielding or if you had double attack, but not without either of those.

And no, I'm not debating that you can or cannot melee.
▲ Page top
RE: meelee
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2003 at 7:24 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591787011091
25 posts
OK, first off shamans CAN meelee... i dun care what anyone says... ima 56 shaman and ive solod almost all of my lvls, and while doing this is TANKED and MEELEE'D alot of the mobs ive killed, best way i do this is, chill on my horsey with KEI, and thats gives me pretty good mana regen right there, not to mention my self buffs... i can give myself some pretty decent stats. i got soo many buffs that can stack right now, i can easily get 2k hp and over 1k atk and 900ac. all this plus my self haste. i can deal some pretty good damg. so no matter what anyone says i kno what i can do and i am a shaman, and proud to be 1 at that!

# Aug 16 2002 at 7:46 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592759189462
14 posts
wow that was nice and arrogant . we dont do important melee damage ? sounds like a warrior :p

Sorry , your wrong . im in the 50's and when i go OOM i can tell you i have more then 1 weapon on me that lets me do enough damage to "help" finish off whatever we are fighting . im NOT debating that we are no warriors . i do debate we have the weapons and the races that give us the strength to "help" when needed.

Besides , i dont need to remind you of rabid bear do i ? ;)

▲ Page top
Shamans and melee
QuoteReply
# Dec 17 2001 at 3:52 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248069069
2 posts
I must agree with those of you who say shamans can melee and melee well. I am a 51 Mystic and I melee often when I am full mana and to aid my pet while soloing. I have a Granite Face Grinder 29/36 10str 10wis 10int and when buffed I hit for over 120 damage against mobs that con blue through L60 in Velketor's Labyrinth. I often do the pulling with Tugur's Insects so I don't get hurt very bad and I cast E-Bolt once the melee is engaged. Combine the two and I often to over 2500 damage in a single encounter. It is not about playing your character this-way or that, but it IS about how you play the way you have chosen.

Grolnirflab Froggiesquisha
51 Troll Mystic, Povar
▲ Page top
another view...
QuoteReply
# Sep 18 2001 at 8:17 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591763263711
2 posts
While it may seem odd to some who play the SHM class like a DRU to see a SHM doing melee, let me assure you that with my Blight Hammer and 197 skill in 2hb I have hit for a high of 95 so far..( 105 with an Icegrinder again, so far..) and can pull mobs off the clerics and chanters in my group and keep them busy until the rest of the melee gets to them or the chanter puts them back to sleep. If all you offer a group is a back up healer, you are not offering enough. I don't melee every fight I don't even melee most fights but, when push gets to shove, I have the weapons skills, ac and hp to get in there and shove back. That has made a difference in many a tight spot.

Baersoul Justicebringer
Shaman of the Tribunal
House Kendan on Karana
52 seasons of bringing Justice to those who desire it least
▲ Page top
RE: another view...
QuoteReply
# May 16 2002 at 3:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1599437907616
View User Forum
23 posts
That is the truth, Bearsoul. Shamen...not just backup healers anymore!

I can honestly say that once I've turgur'd the mob and plagued or e-bolted, I tend to jump into the fight with my blight in hand. And while my 2hb skill is only in the 160s, I still hit for 70+, and with speed of the shisser up, I'm tanking as well as the pallies and SKs I group with! And, of course, we have one of the best taunts in the game...HEAL! So, I can say that I'd love one of these, and will be happy to rid myself of that spine chill spear once I get one.

;)

▲ Page top
RE: another view...
QuoteReply
# Dec 03 2001 at 11:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591751028954
4 posts
Well said Baersoul. My shaman is only level 30 and he can solo tank mobs his level, being hit for 50 damage a pop. With my Fungi Covered Scaled Tunic and Regeneration cast on myself I get 21 HPs every tick, plus my AC is 635 unbuffed.
With stat buffs and Quickness on myself and Slow and STR debuff on mob, they don't stand a chance.
My melee skills are capped for level 29, and I plan on keeping them up. As far as this weapon goes, the del/dam ratio is wicked, and it looks cool to boot. Even though it's 2 handed it's got the same mana factor that my Howling Harpoon and Testament of Vanear have, so I'd definitely like to get one of these. In another 25 levels or so, that is =)
▲ Page top
OK TIME TO CLEAR SOME SHIT UP...
QuoteReply
# Sep 05 2001 at 11:22 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248094746
15 posts
1. yes this weapon is a near pure novelty item.
2. dont even start talking **** im a lvl 59 shm, i have this weapon and i also have epic.

this weapon is one u use as a "status" symbol, this weapon is not easy to get, i did not get it till lvl 58. and at lvl 58 my gear was way better then this lance, i have epic. but i still looted just b/c it looks very cool. and the fact i was the only shm there...

ok now for shamans soloing. if you melee when u solo and u have a JBB your a damn moron! i have an RBB and i wanted to see if with my epic with my casted haste (celerity) and my RBB on if i could do more then the 263dd of the JBB in the casting time (8secs) and its just not possible and also when u JBB u have range on the mob. so, you can either root/dot or you can slow/pet/dot either way... personally i just root park, dogs take a lot of preparing that you could lose quickly if you get in trouble for some reason. also, the slow is just mana that is wasted which results in more down time. and as we all know down time is bad. thus if you have to solo just, malosini pull, root, dot, (with whatever you have) preferably epic / bane of nife / pox of bertoxx and then u can either slap on your fungi and cann or u can just sit and med or even, dare i say it, JBB!. either way, melee is not really an option, especially against higher lvl shaman soloable mobs such as the ones in SF, if u try and melee a damn chromadrake have fun man ill personally laugh at u as your buffs are dispelled and your doted.... (granted not all mobs a lvl 59 shm solos has procs or **** of that sort, yes i solo in seb also, like 3spawn and what not)

granted this only my opinon but u must consider the source i have spent many days playing my shaman and im telling you what is most efficent. and for all you people saying the shaman epic is "epic, meaning takes for ever" thats a load of crap. i have done a guild twinks epic in 1, yes that number is huge a whole 1, day. granted my epic to a while but it was one of the firt epics in the game, back before all the spoilers and all the things like that. the shm epic is easy to get if u have 2 grps of friends willing to help you. the only thing u cant do unless u have more people with you (unless your 2 grps is all lvl 59/60) is do fear. the golems are a lil bit harder then when i had to do my epic.i recommend 18 peeps for fear. other then that the shm epic is a piece of cake. now as far as weps better then epic there is only 1 wep i occasionally see used over epic is the 2hb from VP. even then thats only used when ur not solo.

so my advice to all you up and coming shamans is not to drool over this as a wep you would really use, unless you have some really nice friends who are taking you to get this, then by all means be very excited. but if you have to organize grps for this its not worth it, because by the time your high enough lvl to get to this place with a few grps you WILL have better. that will is capped b/c you should, if you dont your just lazy and never botherd doing epic... which is sad.


im very sure ill get flamers for this post but o well there are alway a ignorant few.

thank you all for your time, i figgerd i would try my best to clear some of this up.

Exzyl lvl 59 shm quellious
▲ Page top
RE: OK TIME TO CLEAR SOME SHIT UP...
QuoteReply
# Sep 12 2002 at 12:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251521438
1 post
Root dot as a shm sucks. For one everything you cast on it Dot, Jbb ect will break root and youwill have to cast again. It is much more mana efficient to slow mob and torpor your-self if you have too. Then use JBB. If you have attack on you will swing between JBB cast for extra dmg. So load up your Jbb, hammer of spirit calling, epic, aleje's cerimonial cudgel, or this Spirit Talker's War Lance and you'll add some noticeable dmg over a fight. JBB hits 263 you add 0-168 melee dmg JBB hit 263, works well.
▲ Page top
RE: OK TIME TO CLEAR SOME SHIT UP...
QuoteReply
# Nov 11 2001 at 6:49 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591762941553
31 posts
Eeerr.. You could have written in one line "I think you'll have better than this when you're high level enough to get this"..

Would have been a lot more coherent and short
▲ Page top
Shaman Tanking
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2001 at 10:56 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Ok im no self professed expert but I do play a 45 shammy and I do tank some not much just some.

1) I'm a wis caster so I have more hp than the int caster so I can take a few hits alot better not to mention if im grouped for xp I'm buffed for hp and manna.

2) Like all casters your gonna get aggoed and the fastest way to loose it is take the hits and let the tanks do there job and remove it from your person. While this is going on I attack in melee heck it's on me anyhow may as well go for a skill hit and add a few points of dammage. Every little bit helps. Not sure if this is a fact but it seems to me that if you attack in melee you get hit less when aggroed. Seems true to me not sure if it is just may be my luck.

3) When the mob is removed from my person I sit back down or keep melee up if i got sit agro still.
▲ Page top
RE: Shaman Tanking
QuoteReply
# Jul 17 2001 at 4:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591759593471
9 posts
I agree with this dude, i play a 45 shaman and more often than not end up duking it out next to my tanks simply because there is no need for me to med any longer than nessicary. if you are in a group with people who know what to do your spells are no more than buffs, debuffs, dots and back up heals.. now what am i supposed to do in between that? i'll tell you what i do.. i kick some azz, as for solo, the only reason i WOULDNT melee during a solo would be if my target is weak enough for my pet to own without me lifting a finger.
▲ Page top
RE: Shaman Tanking
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2001 at 1:31 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361210355
123 posts
O
M
G

So everyone in your group doesn't mind that they have to sit for 10mins after every fight cause you are OOM? And how do you "back-up heal" without mana?
I never cease to be amazed at shaman that melee over medding. And at 45th level no less! Sheesh.
▲ Page top
RE: Shaman Tanking
QuoteReply
# Aug 31 2001 at 9:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758215454
100 posts
I don't recall seeing anywhere in his post about him tanking after he runs outta mana. I think he even states that when he has no need to med he tanks. I play a shaman as well and when I am in a group I slow, buff, and debuff. I rarely heal and only then when we need an immediate heal. Umm, I think Shjekai, you should read the post thoroughly before you flame somone. I do the same thing, if we're fighting and I have full mana and noone needs a buff or instant heal I get up and shove my Spine Chill Spear in the mobs ankle or under it's toenail cause all we seem to hunt are giants now, hehehhe.

I concurr, I tank when I solo, and I tank when I have full mana. Nothing wrong with that, besides the little bit of damage I do helps in ending the fight a little faster which in turn saves the healer some mana because a dead mob cannot beat on our tanks anymore, heheh
▲ Page top
RE: Shaman Tanking
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2001 at 7:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750292295
19 posts
A few options:-

With a nice ratio weapon you can get a hit in when Canni-dancing (careful on agro siting next to mob)

If you are chain nuking with a JBB, you are standing up anyway, so might as well hit something at the same time.

If you are slamming casters

If you are using a weapon with a worthwhile proc, such as a Journeyman's Walking Stick (procs Tashani)


In wierd group situations, such as a Shaman/Rogue/Cleric/Chanter combo.


With C2 I can debuff a mob and be FOM at the end of an average fight while standing up, and contribute 1000+ damage with a JBB + melee damage

Sordain

I could care less what the stats on this weapon are because it looks cool as all get out. Would I use it in battle? No I would not, because I would rather have a shield for the extra AC and stats. However, while taking a casual stroll through Norrath I would like to equip it just so everyone can look at me and say "Now that's style."
P.S. Whoever thinks Shamans don't melee at higher levels are poorly mistaken and probably have never played the class or gave up around lvl 23.

2h Pierce
QuoteReply
# Jun 04 2001 at 10:55 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Someone in this long list of chats said that your 2H Piercing skill is based on your 1H Piercing skill. Well I am a pally and I bought Messenger of the Queen which is 2h piercing and since pallies DONT have 1h piercing its pretty pointless to make 2h piercing for pallies then. In other words, your wrong. =)
▲ Page top
RE: 2h Pierce
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 10:40 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
No, you are wrong.

2h Piercing and 1h Piercing both use the same skill "Piercing".

Paladins were given the skill "Piercing" when Verant introduced these 2h weapons. If there are any 1h Piercers which have PAL on them (and there are most likely some in the database) then they will be equippable by Paladins.

Regards,
Eduin
▲ Page top
...
QuoteReply
# Jun 02 2001 at 12:23 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Just gonna say, all these people are saying that shaman who solo don't go melee. But that's what I thought to, but one of my friends is a level 59 shamy and he melees everytime he solos. With a fungi tunic, regrowth, a pet, and with your enemey slowed, its better to melee and cast then it is to be a wanna be druid. Unless that is you want to spent little mana, in that case you root and keep right clicking that JB Bracer you shamans like so much. Just tryin to help you guys out. Other priest classes dont go melee cuase we dont have the AC (cept for clerics, but they and every other priest class still can't do it cuase of no slow). Anyway, thats my 2 cents.

Eldoth D'Avalon (53 druid)
Yindar Kimic (51 enchanter)
Rakish Daflirt (31 monk)
Veeshan

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 02 2001 at 9:20 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Umm, Shaman don't have 2h Piercing skill.
RE: My only problem
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2001 at 1:20 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The 'Piercing' skill pertains to the whole spectrum or piercing skills. Piercing encompasses 2h piercing as well.

▲ Page top
Dang whiners..
QuoteReply
# May 28 2001 at 4:12 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965850704
67 posts
First of all, all those who are trying to say "historically only knights used lances blah-blah", then remember this - historically frikkin' paladins and shamans didn't cast any spell either, and there was no shadowknights also. And many more things didn't exist - it's a dang fantasy game! So get over it.

These who count here numbers and say how "this and that will give you this stat and that skill and those ratings" - go back to your boring RL, nerds. Damn accountants. Can't keep their boring number-talk to themselves and then come here with their BS talk about numbers and efficiency.

Sheesh, people are pathetic, whining on every turn about everything. If you are a shaman and have it and will use it, then do so. If you are a shaman and do not have it, or can't have it, then don't whine and try to discourage those who have it. If you are not a shaman at all, then just shut up. It isn't for your class, so there's nothing for you to whine about here. Such whining is simply a sign of jealousy, common greed. "Waah I am a wizard and I don't have such lance!". So? You don't have it, then don't ballistic on those who have it. You have your own trinkets and you will get your own thingies, which will be more useful to you.

Why can't the dang people just post something useful as the comments? Like where to get all the items listed, suggestions on places/situations where to use, etc. Why has it always come down to whining and greedy/jealous messages?

If you don't have anything useful to say, then just don't say anything.
▲ Page top

Um....Its even worse when people WHINE louder than the "whiners". I didn't hear anything useful in your whole post...I guess you shouldn't have said anything at all.

▲ Page top
Nice, but.. Hmm
QuoteReply
# May 28 2001 at 1:07 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757381256
26 posts
I like this weapon, and personally, it's like any substandard female.. Wouldn't kick it outa the bed, but not gonna wet my pants over it. Fact is, For cheapness, you can get more Wis, and a WHOLE lot more AC at a much ealier time (I.e. Paw of Oppola+Charred Guardian Shield) I understand that's an old school idea.. but, it also comes down to this, I play a 53rd Shaman, Proximity=Success on Slows. Slow is a super Taunt,especially if ya move in close to maximize yer chances on say, a Shroom in Seb. AC is helpful since we do spoon agro from Chanters and such a lot, we NEED to be able to take a hit to the head, hell that was the main appeal of the class for me initially, the line saying a caster who wasn't afraid to take hit in the noggin. A lot of it does really depend on preference though, I play a Barb, and since I got my JBB, I don't melee much (Go figure) But, I still know Shamans who swear by 2HB, 60s using Blight still.. so I can imagine seeing this one used a bit as well. It is pimp lookin that's for sure. I can see wearring it and the full Rune Etched in EC just for the look, but I myself doubt I'd use it much in combat situations, I'd prefer Garduk over this honestly. But is still not a bad offering from VI.
▲ Page top
Weapons Better than epic
QuoteReply
# May 27 2001 at 9:55 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
To the person that said it would be stupid for weapons to drop off an NPC that are BETTER than a class specific weapon... Whether it be stupid or not. It happens.
Look at the Primal weapons in Sleepers Tomb. They are War/Pal/SK, and they blow away every single one of their epic weapons. Let me get stats so i don't get flamed because you don't know about them...
http://eqdb.allakhazam.com/itemsearch.html?terms=primal
should show you all the primal weapons this site has in their database. i'll pick one tho.
****
Primal Velium Battle Axe
2hs 33dmg 35 dly
10ac 5 to all stats (including resists)
50hp 50mana
WAR/SK/PAL/RNG
Proc Avatar
****
Avatar = 100str, 100 dex, 100agl, 100 stam, 100 atk.
But the 100 str gives an addition 100 atk or so. SO you actually gain 200 atk off it.

In short. There ARE better weapons than epics. I'd bet you there are shaman weapons in ST that blow away their epic. Maybe not found yet, as not many have done ST (not on my server anyways.).
Enjoy! Have some background before you post plz :)
▲ Page top
Primal Vel are awesome ;)
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2001 at 5:32 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
just to add to this post, i read a awesome tactic under "primal velium spear" it is to ballance Str,dex,agl at 160 (as most people try to go high on these stats for me'lee) and insted use these slots for AC, you proc avatar, boom, 255 255 255 awesome AC,..
thats gotta scare the mobs *grins*

Cilas shadowfoot
23 Rogue
Zeb

Avatar doesn't give any +sta, and the combination of 100 atk and 100 str is only at most about 160-180 atk extra (less, since most people with primals have a base str of at least 200).

#Anonymous, Posted: May 27 2001 at 3:22 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Why is it always number crunching with you geeks?!? Its a pimp looking weapon, and if I was a Shaman I would try to get one EVEN if I had my epic. Walking through EC tunnel, I'd show off that cool looking lance which is HUGE and simply PIMP. So for you pencil pushing number crunching people that have nothing better to do then count stats, play the fuggin game how it should be played!

ok im not a shammy, nor do i wish to be one bcuz of the down time that they get. however, for the ppl that say this weapon sucks, plz tell me a weapon that is shaman only with these kind of stats that is not the epic for the shamans, of course the epic weapon will be better, it would be stupid of verant to sit there and make epic weapon, worse than a weapon droped off of a npc. so the point of this is this thing is tits compared to any of the other shaman only weapons, EXCEPT the epic, because the epic quest is supposed to be the best weapon for the paritcular class so who ever says this weapon sucks you are either A). a oxy moron, or B). just plane stupid
